name: SGLang
pricing_notes: Open source; self-hosted on your own GPU infrastructure

auth.notes: Optional API key when running SGLang server; OpenAI-compatible REST API endpoint

performance.notes: Significantly faster than vLLM on many workloads; RadixAttention KV cache reuse; up to 5x higher throughput vs HuggingFace

agent_readiness.notes: OpenAI-compatible API server + Python frontend DSL. RadixAttention for KV cache sharing across requests. Constrained decoding for JSON/regex output. Tool calling supported. Faster than vLLM on batch inference.

gotchas: GPU required — not suitable for CPU-only or low-RAM environments
Setup more complex than Ollama for single-user scenarios
Documentation for advanced features (constrained decoding) sparse
Production deployment and scaling requires infrastructure expertise
Less model coverage than vLLM for bleeding-edge releases
best_when: High-throughput batch inference for LLM workloads on GPU
Need structured JSON output with constrained decoding
Running multi-turn agent workflows where KV cache reuse is valuable
avoid_when: Need managed inference (use vLLM-based providers or cloud APIs)
summary: SGLang (Structured Generation Language) is a high-performance LLM inference framework with a unique DSL for structured generation and RadixAttention for KV cache reuse across requests. Benchmarks show 2-5x higher throughput than vLLM on many workloads. OpenAI-compatible API server. Best choice for performance-critical self-hosted inference with batch workloads.
